source: Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) — top holdings cross-referenced with S&P 500 IT sector membership. Composed from stockanalysis.com top-25 (NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, AVGO, MU, AMD, CSCO, PLTR, LRCX, AMAT, INTC, ORCL, TXN, KLAC, IBM, ADI, APH, ANET, CRM, QCOM, PANW, SNDK, WDC, GLW, STX) plus confident long-tenured XLK constituents — not a full daily holdings replica but a representative concentrated-mega-tech basket for autoresearch.
survivorship_warning: Frozen 2026 snapshot of XLK-style large-cap US tech holdings. Survivorship bias is meaningful but lower than XBI: SP500 IT sector adds/drops are slow, and the dominant names (Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, Broadcom) have been mega-cap for the entire 2010-2024 IS+OOS span. Two distortions to flag: (1) the universe is heavily skewed by the post-2017 GICS reclassification that moved Facebook/Alphabet/etc. into Communication Services — those names are NOT here despite being 'tech' in spirit; (2) names like PLTR, SMCI, ARM IPO'd late, so their pre-2020 history is missing from the parquet. The universe leans into the FAANG+ trend persistence story; trend-following / momentum results here should be read as 'this works on the slice of US tech that survived as mega-cap', not 'this works on tech broadly'.
